instruction:
Write a complete testthat test file for `stringr::to_words`.

Package: stringr v1.6.0.9000
Description: Simple, Consistent Wrappers for Common String Operations
Function: to_words
Parameters: string
Test patterns used: test_that
Expectations used: expect_equal

Function Code:
```r
to_words <- function(string) {
  breakpoints <- paste(
    # non-word characters
    "[^\\p{L}\\p{N}]+",
    # lowercase followed by uppercase
    "(?<=\\p{Ll})(?=\\p{Lu})",
    # letter followed by number
    "(?<=\\p{L})(?=\\p{N})",
    # number followed by letter
    "(?<=\\p{N})(?=\\p{L})",
    # uppercase followed uppercase then lowercase (i.e. end of acronym)
    "(?<=\\p{Lu})(?=\\p{Lu}\\p{Ll})",
    sep = "|"
  )
  out <- str_replace_all(string, breakpoints, " ")
  out <- str_to_lower(out)
  str_trim(out)
}
```

Requirements:
1. Use test_that() blocks with descriptive names that reference `to_words`
2. Test basic functionality using appropriate expectations (expect_equal)
3. Test edge cases: NULL input, empty strings, missing arguments
4. Test error conditions with expect_error()
5. Each test_that() block should test one logical behavior
